meta.title: MergeCore Alpine.js Rules
meta.description: Rules for Alpine.js in Blade and front-end assets: safe state, lists, async, effects, and Livewire interop.
meta.notes: Gate with alpine: true when the host supports project metadata. Rules marked alpine: true are Alpine-specific.

scoring.normalisation: Per review unit (one Blade file or one JS module with Alpine), sum penalties for matched rules after optional quality_signal multiplier, then score = max(0, min(10, initial_score - min(sum_penalties, max_total_penalty_per_file))).

alpine_overrides.description: Optional host tuning: multiply penalties for category 'security' and 'correctness' when the file has many x-on or x-model bindings.

interpretation.9_to_10: Safe x-data, keyed lists, handled async, clear $wire usage.
interpretation.7_to_8: Shippable; tighten a few client boundaries or extract scripts.
interpretation.5_to_6: Risky: unsafe initial state, missing keys, or fragile effects.
interpretation.0_to_4: Block: injectable x-data, or broken interop with Livewire.

rules.title: Alpine x-data built from unescaped user input
rules.description: Concatenating `{!! !!}` output or raw JSON from users into x-data can break out of the string context and execute code. Use @js() on server-shaped data or static Alpine initialisers.

heuristics: User model field inside x-data without @js

rules.title: x-for without stable :key
rules.description: When lists reorder, filter, or replace items, Alpine needs a :key on the template so the DOM reconciles correctly. Omitting :key causes duplicate UI and lost focus.

rules.title: Very large inline script in a Blade view
rules.description: Hundreds of lines of JavaScript in a Blade file is hard to test and lint. Prefer Vite modules, Alpine.data(), or dedicated JS files.

heuristics: Single Blade file exceeds ~300 lines with most content inside script tags

rules.title: Parallel x-model and wire:model without @entangle
rules.description: Two-way binding the same conceptual value with x-model and wire:model leads to drift and lost updates. Use @entangle('field').live or a single owner.

heuristics: Same variable name in both directives

rules.title: x-init async work without loading or error state
rules.description: fetch() or other promises in x-init without setting loading/error flags leaves the UI stuck or silent on failure.

heuristics: x-init contains await fetch without catch UI

rules.title: x-effect with unclear or leaky side effects
rules.description: x-effect that mutates unrelated DOM, global state, or fires network calls on every reactive tick is hard to reason about. Prefer explicit events or narrow dependencies.

heuristics: Body calls fetch or document.querySelector without guard

examples.after: Call tracking from x-on:click or a dedicated method with a single trigger

rules.title: $wire used without defensive checks
rules.description: Calling $wire methods before Livewire has mounted or without optional chaining can throw in SSR or during teardown. Guard with $wire existence or use @entangle when possible.

heuristics: Runs in x-on without @click on Livewire-managed element

examples.after: x-on:click="$wire && $wire.save()" or ensure element is inside @livewire component

rules.title: Strong pattern: Alpine.data or module for behaviour
rules.description: Registering Alpine.data('name', factory) or importing a small module keeps behaviour testable and reusable across Blade partials.

heuristics: Factory returns object with clear methods
